112137.explanation: The car-restricted plus area must be reversed. Residents lose their parking spots and must move to garages meant for visitors. This increases parking pressure, makes the city less accessible, and harms entrepreneurs throughout the city.

112139.explanation: Delft is not a student district. New student housing belongs on the campus or outside the city. PVV Delft wants to keep room for families, starters, and seniors. The housing balance in the city must be restored.

112140.explanation: Companies that started on the TU campus fall under the responsibility of the TU. PVV Delft is against subsidizing individual companies. The municipality must ensure a good business climate for all entrepreneurs, not make special deals.

112141.explanation: PVV Delft opposes continuing the district heating network. It is expensive, unreliable, and limits residents' freedom of choice. People should decide themselves how to heat their homes, without coercion.

112080.explanation: A good city government represents all human and non-human inhabitants of Delft, regardless of their gender, origin, or circumstances.

112081.explanation: PvdD takes the effort to consult residents. It reduces the chance of costly mistakes and increases the chance of citizen involvement.

112084.explanation: Peace and dark nights are important for the well-being of people and animals. Nature conservation also concerns a healthy urban environment.

112086.explanation: People without valid documents also have human rights. They must have access to (medical) care and be able to use the shelter, bath, and bread facilities.

112090.explanation: We support breathtaking New Year's shows using innovative technology. Fireworks seem fun, but they mainly destroy a lot of life.

112093.explanation: The municipality sets the standard, leads by example, and provides support, but entrepreneurs themselves must pay to make their business inclusive and accessible.

112097.explanation: Growing up with culture and music helps children build a strong foundation for life. These costs are an investment in a healthy future.

112098.explanation: We choose a strong club life. If help is needed, the municipality will step in. However, no unlimited financial guarantees will be given to sports clubs.

112099.explanation: We want the municipality to promote the repurposing of monuments. Demolition of culturally or historically valuable or iconic buildings must be prevented. Whether this costs more or less money must be determined.

112100.explanation: Safety is possible in a healthy living environment and with compassion for one another. We focus on visible prevention of street problems and on 'green enforcement officers': special constables with expert knowledge of nature and environmental protection in and around Delft.

112101.explanation: Cameras do not provide real security. If necessary, the municipality must handle our privacy very carefully. Use must be temporary and under strict conditions, such as a maximum retention period for footage.

112103.explanation: Enforcement officers in the city should not be armed with batons, but with extensive people skills. Only police officers and specialized rural enforcement officers have access to weapons.

112107.explanation: We support high fines for vacant properties. Through a vacancy ordinance with sanctions, we encourage long-term vacant buildings to be made habitable. This is done at the owner's expense, not the community's.

112108.explanation: In a win-win situation, the municipality can help with the temporary housing of students.

112109.explanation: Meaningful work, paid or unpaid, provides stability in life. Mandatory participation does not work positively. PvdD believes in supporting people to contribute to society.

112110.explanation: Municipal taxes are not proportional to a person's income. People with little money really cannot afford it.

112111.explanation: We support expanding the minimum policy to incomes up to 130% of the social minimum. This includes, for example, special social assistance.

112114.explanation: We support more charging points with green energy for electric vehicles. The switch to clean transport should be easy.

112116.explanation: We support paid parking in public spaces. Firstly, because this space is valuable and shared by all, and secondly, because we want to discourage car ownership.

112117.explanation: Tailored transportation is sustainable, social, and safe. It is a necessary supplement to public transport and ensures a livable city for everyone.

112118.explanation: We support a zero-emission zone for commercial traffic in the city center.

112119.explanation: The number of cars in the municipality can and must be reduced. We can regulate this, among other things, with parking permits.

112120.explanation: We choose care that aligns with the individual. Where reasonable, care providers consider faith, culture, and identity. However, the care offered is determined by quality, not by someone's beliefs or culture.

112121.explanation: Youth care is about providing the right care. Knowing which care is appropriate prevents many costs for the municipality and additional problems for the client. The cheapest way of youth care is selecting based on quality and generous prevention.

112124.explanation: Tourism can cause damage to the climate and nature through additional transport and overconsumption. The higher revenues from tourist tax must be used for sustainability and strengthening nature.

112125.explanation: We do not want more business parks, but we do focus on smarter and greener design of existing parks. We offer this space to sustainable, non-polluting companies or change the zoning plan to nature and housing.

112126.explanation: In Delft, all shops may already be open on Sundays.

112127.explanation: Everyone is treated equally when assigning rental homes. By building flexible homes, the housing shortage can be addressed in the short term for people with the highest urgency.

112129.explanation: New construction of a city theater offers many opportunities for Delft. PvdD supports a sustainable and nature-inclusive design that is multifunctional and where daily creative interaction takes place.

112132.explanation: There are enough coffeeshops in Delft. The current coffeeshops are closely monitored for compliance with local policy. PvdD also commits to a health-oriented approach and prevention.

112135.explanation: We want students and young adults to be able to live affordably in shared housing, and the possibilities for this should be expanded.

112137.explanation: Public spaces must prioritize pedestrians, cyclists, and public transport.

112139.explanation: New construction in Delft must be built for people who want to stay living in Delft long-term.

112140.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be financially healthy themselves and not dependent on municipal subsidies. Delft can provide favorable conditions for start-ups and scale-ups by streamlining municipal permit procedures.

112141.explanation: The Warmtenet Delft offers a sustainable and affordable alternative to heating with gas. However, the activities must not harm nature.

statements.title: New student housing should be located outside the city, on campus and in surrounding municipalities.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Delft should renovate Theater de Veste.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Delft should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
There should be more camera surveillance in Delft.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: The BOAs who check safety in Delft should be given a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Delft controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Parking at shopping centers must remain free.
The municipality should financially help companies established on the TU campus to stay in Delft.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet bedrijven die op de campus van de TU zijn opgericht

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om in Delft te blijven.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Delft should continue to provide shelter for rejected asylum seekers.
There should be more business parks in Delft.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent the opening of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shops or cafés where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should invest in tailored transport between the local centers (for example a dial‑a‑ride bus).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in

titleParts.glossary: A type of public transport that doesn't run on a fixed schedule but can be called to pick you up.
A village or small town that is part of the municipality.
A bus that only runs if you reserve it in advance. The bus then takes you door to door.

statements.title: The residents of Delft should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Delft moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should continue the rollout of the district heating network.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de aanleg van het

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.

shootoutStatements.title: Delft should introduce a low-emission zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the city center.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should cut spending on heritage conservation.
Delft should introduce a parking permit system in more neighborhoods.
The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.
